refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall ensure teleoperation end-to-end communication latency p95 ≤ 150 ms under agreed network conditions to enable safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is decomposed to explicitly state the latency metric and its impact on safety, focusing on communication latency as the critical factor for teleoperation safety.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure end-to-end latency of teleoperation commands and feedback under controlled network conditions and verify p95 latency ≤ 150 ms.
sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency between operator and vehicle.
FR2: Ensure reliable network link to support latency requirements.
FR3: Provide real-time telemetry and tasking data with minimal delay.

candidate_dps: DP1: Use optimized network protocols and compression to reduce latency.
DP2: Implement edge computing nodes to process commands closer to vehicle.
DP3: Prioritize teleoperation traffic on network link with QoS mechanisms.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Use optimized network protocols and compression to reduce latency.

potential_sources: Interaction between network protocol optimization and telemetry data format.
Dependency on network link quality and safety services.
coupling_check.notes: DP1 logically addresses latency reduction independently; coupling with other DPs is manageable with clear interface definitions.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions remain within agreed parameters.
Risk of protocol overhead causing unexpected latency spikes.
Risk that compression may degrade telemetry data quality.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating edge computing (DP2) if latency targets are not met with protocol optimization alone.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Realization of teleoperation communication protocols, latency optimization algorithms, and command/feedback handling.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Physical and logical network connection supporting teleoperation data exchange.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Real-time vehicle status and sensor data streaming interface.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Command and control interface for teleoperation tasks.

module_containment_scores.notes: Broad scope but less focused on teleoperation specifics; moderate containment.
Vehicle module primarily hardware-focused; low containment for communication latency.
Highest containment score; directly responsible for teleoperation communication and latency.
Supports safety alerts but not primary for latency control.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Latency optimization logic and communication protocols are best contained within the Teleoperation module to minimize diffusion and maximize ownership clarity.
containment_risks: Potential overlap with Safety Services for safety-critical latency monitoring.
Dependency on Network Link interface quality and availability.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Define clear interface contracts with Safety Services and Network Link to mitigate containment risks.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP logically satisfies the refined FR with clear latency reduction focus and manageable coupling.

diffusion_risks: Some diffusion risk exists if latency optimization logic is duplicated outside Teleoperation module.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Teleoperation module shows strong containment for DP; interfaces are well defined.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Latency can be measured end-to-end through Teleoperation module interfaces using network monitoring tools.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of latency responsibility to Teleoperation module.

recommended_revisions: Clarify interface contracts with Safety Services for latency monitoring and alerts.
Monitor potential protocol overhead impact on telemetry data quality.

decision.justification: The current level of decomposition and assignments meet the requirements with clear FR refinement, DP selection, and IM assignment. Verification and ownership are well defined with manageable risks.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency between operator and vehicle.
FR2: Ensure reliable network link to support latency requirements.
FR3: Provide real-time telemetry and tasking data with minimal delay.

revise.why: No immediate revision needed; proceed to next decomposition level.

assumptions: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters.
Teleoperation module can implement required protocol optimizations.
evidence_needed: Latency measurement data under test network conditions.
Interface performance metrics between Teleoperation and Safety Services.
